msgid: Continues processing after a checksum failure.
draft_msgstr: [REVIEW: untranslated] Continues processing after a checksum failure.

msgid: Detection of a checksum failure normally causes PostgreSQL to report an error, aborting the current transaction. Setting ignore_checksum_failure to true causes the system to ignore the failure (but still report a warning), and continue processing. This behavior could cause crashes or other serious problems. Only has an effect if checksums are enabled.
draft_msgstr: [REVIEW: transaction; sentence] Detection of a checksum failure normally causes PostgreSQL to report an त्रुटि, aborting the current transaction. Setting ignore_checksum_failure to true causes the system to ignore the failure (but still report a चेतावनी), and continue processing. This behavior could cause crashes or other serious problems. Only has an effect if checksums are enabled.

msgid: Continues processing past damaged page headers.
draft_msgstr: [REVIEW: untranslated] Continues processing past damaged page headers.

msgid: Detection of a damaged page header normally causes PostgreSQL to report an error, aborting the current transaction. Setting "zero_damaged_pages" to true causes the system to instead report a warning, zero out the damaged page, and continue processing. This behavior will destroy data, namely all the rows on the damaged page.
draft_msgstr: [REVIEW: transaction; sentence] Detection of a damaged page header normally causes PostgreSQL to report an त्रुटि, aborting the current transaction. Setting "zero_damaged_pages" to true causes the system to instead report a चेतावनी, zero out the damaged page, and continue processing. This behavior will destroy data, नामly all the rows on the damaged page.

msgid: Continues recovery after an invalid pages failure.
draft_msgstr: [REVIEW: recovery] Continues recovery after an अमान्य pages failure.

msgid: Detection of WAL records having references to invalid pages during recovery causes PostgreSQL to raise a PANIC-level error, aborting the recovery. Setting "ignore_invalid_pages" to true causes the system to ignore invalid page references in WAL records (but still report a warning), and continue recovery. This behavior may cause crashes, data loss, propagate or hide corruption, or other serious problems. Only has an effect during recovery or in standby mode.
draft_msgstr: [REVIEW: recovery, standby, WAL; sentence] Detection of WAL records having references to अमान्य pages during recovery causes PostgreSQL to raise a PANIC-level त्रुटि, aborting the recovery. Setting "ignore_invalid_pages" to true causes the system to ignore अमान्य page references in WAL records (but still report a चेतावनी), and continue recovery. This behavior may cause crashes, data loss, propagate or hide corruption, or other serious problems. Only has an effect during recovery or in standby mode.

msgid: A page write in process during an operating system crash might be only partially written to disk.  During recovery, the row changes stored in WAL are not enough to recover.  This option writes pages when first modified after a checkpoint to WAL so full recovery is possible.
draft_msgstr: [REVIEW: checkpoint, recovery, WAL; untranslated] A page write in process during an operating system crash might be only partially written to disk.  During recovery, the row changes stored in WAL are not enough to recover.  This option writes pages when first modified after a checkpoint to WAL so full recovery is possible.
